title: TDS Responsive Rules Manifest
purpose: AI-cache of the token-driven responsive contract: breakpoint tokens, device tiers, Storybook viewports, and the useMediaQuery hook contract. Lets agents reason about responsive behavior without re-reading src/tokens or src/hooks. Breakpoints are DERIVED from real tokens; a formal responsive layout system beyond the hook + tokens is PARTIAL/PLANNED.
precedence: Derived cache. The authoritative sources are src/tokens/primitives.ts (breakpoint scale) and src/hooks/index.ts (useMediaQuery). On any conflict, the source wins and this manifest MUST be regenerated. Never edit generated token/CSS outputs to change breakpoints — change the source token and re-run npm run tokens:build.
designLock: Breakpoint values, device tiers, and viewport sizes are visual-design decisions locked by the Design Lock Policy. An AI MUST NEVER add, remove, rename, or re-value a breakpoint on its own initiative. Any change is a SOURCE change (src/tokens/primitives.ts) that flows through npm run ds:build and must be explicitly requested/approved.

model.note: All breakpoints are authored as dimension tokens under the 'Breakpoint' group. Token id dot-notation maps to CSS var (--tds-breakpoint-<name>) and to a Figma variable name (breakpoint/<name>). px values below are the resolved token scale from src/tokens/primitives.ts; the token — not the number — is authoritative.

deviceTiers.description: Phones. Base (default) layer in the mobile-first cascade — no media query required.
Tablets / large phones landscape. Anchored to breakpoint.tablet.
Large / wide desktop monitors. Anchored to breakpoint.wide.

mediaQueryPatterns.note: Canonical query strings to pass into useMediaQuery. Values mirror the breakpoint tokens; regenerate if the token scale changes.

behavior: Returns false on the initial render (SSR-safe default), then resolves to the real match inside a useEffect via window.matchMedia(query).
Subscribes to the MediaQueryList 'change' event and re-renders on viewport changes; unsubscribes on unmount.
Accepts any valid CSS media-query string — callers SHOULD pass strings from mediaQueryPatterns rather than hardcoding px.

storybookViewports.note: Preview viewport presets used for responsive review in Storybook (source: .storybook/preview.tsx). These are review canvases and are distinct from the breakpoint tokens, though tablet/wide align with them.

rules: MUST treat breakpoint tokens (breakpoint.mobile/tablet/desktop/wide) as the single source of truth for responsive thresholds.
MUST NOT hardcode pixel breakpoints in components, CSS, or media queries — reference the breakpoint token / CSS var (--tds-breakpoint-*) or a query string from mediaQueryPatterns.
MUST author responsive CSS mobile-first using min-width queries; the mobile tier is the un-queried base layer.
MUST use useMediaQuery from @/hooks for JS-driven responsive branching; MUST NOT call window.matchMedia or window.innerWidth directly in components.
MUST account for the SSR-safe default (useMediaQuery returns false on first render) and avoid layout that flashes on hydration.
SHOULD keep component responsiveness purely token-driven so the same thresholds reproduce in Figma layout grids / constraints.
NEVER add, rename, or re-value a breakpoint here — change src/tokens/primitives.ts and re-run npm run tokens:build; this manifest is regenerated, never hand-edited.
NEVER let Figma or this cache introduce responsive rules absent from the Storybook source (Design Lock).

figmaMapping.note: Breakpoints are emitted to the Figma bundle as dimension Variables (breakpoint/*) in the Primitives collection. Full responsive reproduction (layout grids / constraints keyed to these variables) is PLANNED; the state axis and responsive behavior are NOT expanded into Figma variants. See figma/README.md and 05_FIGMA_SPECIFICATION.md.

howChecked: npm run tokens:build regenerates --tds-breakpoint-* in src/tokens/generated/tokens.css and breakpoint/* in figma.tokens.json — breakpoints[] here MUST match those outputs.
npm run lint / tsc -b — useMediaQuery signature and @/hooks import must resolve.
Schema check: every breakpoints[].tokenId MUST exist in src/tokens/generated/token-ids.ts.
Manual: deviceTiers ranges MUST be contiguous and derived from breakpoints[] minWidthPx.
validation.regenerate: npm run ds:build (tokens:build + manifest:build + catalog:build), then re-seed .ai manifests.
